identificationModule.briefTitle: Effectiveness of Tele-exercise Training on Cardiorespiratory Fitness in Patients With Cardiometabolic Multimorbidity
identificationModule.officialTitle: Tri-Service General Hospital, National Defense Medical Center

responsibleParty.investigatorTitle: Associate Professor and Deputy Director of Nursing Department

collaborators.name: Ministry of Science and Technology, Taiwan

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This trial aimed to assess whether assumed increase in physical activity after tele-exercise training can improve cardiorespiratory fitness of patients with cardiometabolic multimorbidity.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription: A parallel-group randomized controlled trial was conducted. Eligible patients with cardiometabolic multimorbidity were randomized 1:1 to either experimental group (EG, received a tele-exercise training program) or control group (CG, usual care only). The intervention encompassed tele-exercise training program (30 min/session, 3 sessions/week) and weekly remote monitoring for maintenance of exercise. Physical activity and cardiorespiratory fitness were assessed at baseline and 12 weeks. Generalized estimating equations were used to examine the intervention effects via the interaction of time and group.

maskingInfo.maskingDescription: Eligible participants were invited to data collection including sociodemographic characteristics and outcome measures in a local medical centre at baseline and 12 weeks by a separate research nurse, blinded to the group assignments.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline physical activity amount at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: weekly amount of physical activity using the well-validated and reliable Taiwan version of 7-item International Physical Activity Questionnaire to measure. The scores of International Physical Activity Questionnaire expressed as "MET-minutes/week". Higher score indicates higher physical activity amounts

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline VO2 peak (ml/kg/min) at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: VO2 peak (ml/kg/min), detected during graded exercise testing, performed using a motorized ergometer (Corival, Lode, Netherlands) with a standardized ramp protocol by increasing the resistance (10 watt per minute) at a consistent speed of 60 revolutions per minute.

Workload (Watts), detected during graded exercise testing, performed using a motorized ergometer (Corival, Lode, Netherlands) with a standardized ramp protocol by increasing the resistance (10 watt per minute) at a consistent speed of 60 revolutions per minute.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline anaerobic threshold at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: anaerobic threshold, detected during graded exercise testing, performed using a motorized ergometer (Corival, Lode, Netherlands) with a standardized ramp protocol by increasing the resistance (10 watt per minute) at a consistent speed of 60 revolutions per minute.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline O2 pulse (ml/beat) at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: O2 pulse (ml/beat), detected during graded exercise testing, performed using a motorized ergometer (Corival, Lode, Netherlands) with a standardized ramp protocol by increasing the resistance (10 watt per minute) at a consistent speed of 60 revolutions per minute.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline One-min heart rate recovery (beat) at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: One-min heart rate recovery (beat), detected during graded exercise testing, performed using a motorized ergometer (Corival, Lode, Netherlands) with a standardized ramp protocol by increasing the resistance (10 watt per minute) at a consistent speed of 60 revolutions per minute.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline Two-min heart rate recovery (beat) at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: Two-min heart rate recovery (beat), detected during graded exercise testing, performed using a motorized ergometer (Corival, Lode, Netherlands) with a standardized ramp protocol by increasing the resistance (10 watt per minute) at a consistent speed of 60 revolutions per minute.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline resting heart rate (bpm) at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: Resting heart rate (bpm) obtained using electronic BP monitor devices (Terumo, ESP2000)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline Resting Systolic Blood Pressure (mmHg) at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: Resting Systolic Blood Pressure (mmHg) obtained using electronic BP monitor devices (Terumo, ESP2000)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline Resting Diastolic Blood Pressure (mmHg) at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: Resting Diastolic Blood Pressure (mmHg), obtained using electronic BP monitor devices (Terumo, ESP2000)

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline forced vital capacity (L/min) at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: forced vital capacity (L/min), detected during graded exercise testing, performed using a motorized ergometer (Corival, Lode, Netherlands) with a standardized ramp protocol by increasing the resistance (10 watt per minute) at a consistent speed of 60 revolutions per minute.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes form baseline forced expiratory volume in one second (L/min) at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: forced expiratory volume in one second (L/min), detected during graded exercise testing, performed using a motorized ergometer (Corival, Lode, Netherlands) with a standardized ramp protocol by increasing the resistance (10 watt per minute) at a consistent speed of 60 revolutions per minute.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline FEV1/FVC (%) at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: FEV1/FVC (%) detected during graded exercise testing, , performed using a motorized ergometer (Corival, Lode, Netherlands) with a standardized ramp protocol by increasing the resistance (10 watt per minute) at a consistent speed of 60 revolutions per minute.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline Ejection fraction (%) at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: Ejection fraction (%) data were collected through chart review

primaryOutcomes.measure: Changes from baseline BNP at 12 weeks
Changes from baseline Health-related quality of life at 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.description: Health-related quality of life useing the reliable and valid Taiwan version of Medical Outcomes Study Short-Form 36 to measure. Scores ranged from 0 to 100, with higher scores indicating better health-related quality of life.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Confirmed diagnoses of cardiometabolic multimorbidity, defined as the presence of two or more cardiometabolic conditions (i.e., hypertension, type 2 diabetes, hyperlipidaemia, heart disease, metabolic syndrome, or gout)
* Aged 18-80 years
* Able to speak and understand Mandarin
* Able to walk without assistance
* Had mobile phones and could easily access the Internet

Exclusion Criteria:

* Not match the diagnosis of cardiometabolic multimorbidity; and a history of arrhythmia, cancer, end-stage renal disease with dialysis, being diagnosed with mental illness, recent stroke within 6 months
* Not able to speak and understand Mandarin
* Not able to walk independently or inability to participate due to comorbid neurological, musculoskeletal conditions that produce moderate-to-severe physical disability
* Did not have mobile phones or could not access the internet at home and whenever necessary
